content:
{
  "message": "Error on the terminal",
  "terminaloutput": "",
  "terminalerror": "Command failed: powershell -Command "Get-CimInstance Win32_Process | Where-Object { $_.Name -match 'brave.exe|chrome.exe|msedge.exe' -and $_.CommandLine -match 'youtube' } | ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_.ProcessId -Force }  & 'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe' --new-window https://www.youtube.com'
At line:1 char:195
+ ...  | ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_.ProcessId -Force }  & 'C:\Pro ...
+                                                                 ~
The ampersand (&) character is not allowed. The & operator is reserved for future use; wrap an ampersand in double quotation marks ("&") to pass 
it as part of a string.
At line:1 char:289
+ ... \Chrome\Application\chrome.exe' --new-window https://www.youtube.com'
+                                                                         ~
The string is missing the terminator: '.
    + CategoryInfo          : ParserError: (:) [], ParentContainsErrorRecordException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : AmpersandNotAllowed
 
",
  "cmdRunByAi": "powershell -Command "Get-CimInstance Win32_Process | Where-Object { $_.Name -match 'brave.exe|chrome.exe|msedge.exe' -and $_.CommandLine -match 'youtube' } | ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_.ProcessId -Force }  & 'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe' --new-window https://www.youtube.com'"
}

{
  "message": "Error on the terminal",
  "terminaloutput": "",
  "terminalerror": "Command failed: powershell -Command "Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Process -Filter "CommandLine likes '*youtube*'"); Get-Process -Name Chrome -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_.Id -Force }"
At line:1 char:79
+ ... lassName Win32_Process -Filter CommandLine likes '*youtube*'); Get-Pr ...
+                                                                 ~
Unexpected token ')' in expression or statement.
    + CategoryInfo          : ParserError: (:) [], ParentContainsErrorRecordException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : UnexpectedToken
 
",
  "cmdRunByAi": "powershell -Command "Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Process -Filter "CommandLine likes '*youtube*'"); Get-Process -Name Chrome -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_.Id -Force }""
}

{
  "message": "Error on the terminal",
  "terminaloutput": "",
  "terminalerror": "Command failed: powershell -Command "& 'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe' --new-window "https://www.youtube.com"
& : The term 'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe' is not recognized as the name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or 
operable program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again.
At line:1 char:3
+ & 'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe' --new-windo ...
+   ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : ObjectNotFound: (C:\Program File...tion\chrome.exe:String) [], CommandNotFoundException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : CommandNotFoundException
 
",
  "cmdRunByAi": "powershell -Command "& 'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe' --new-window "https://www.youtube.com""
}

{
  "message": "Error on the terminal",
  "terminaloutput": "",
  "terminalerror": "Command failed: powershell -Command "start chrome https://www.youtube.com"
start : This command cannot be run due to the error: The system cannot find the file specified.
At line:1 char:1
+ start chrome https://www.youtube.com
+ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : InvalidOperation: (:) [Start-Process], InvalidOperationException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : InvalidOperationException,Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.StartProcessCommand
 
",
  "cmdRunByAi": "powershell -Command "start chrome https://www.youtube.com""
}
